concept: Night / Time of Darkness / Period of Rest
description: The period of darkness between sunset and sunrise; the time for sleep. 'Shero' names the dark hours—rest's domain, dream's realm, the earth turned from sun.
sound_symbolism: The word descends into dark. 'She' /ʃe/ begins with soft breath, light fading. Then 'ro' rolls into depth, night settling.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'mua shero' (at night). Opposite of day in the fundamental light-dark cycle. Time for rest, dreams, and restoration.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Night commanded rest—without artificial light, darkness organized life around sleep.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Night offers different awareness—the quiet mind, the dream state, the unconscious processing.
